body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (untested live — see caveat); driver_capability: gap:slash-command

## Scenario

checkpoint-rewind: a session runs two turns, then the user rewinds the conversation to an earlier checkpoint and continues from there. User-observable signals (scenario-meanings): the session row stays in ready while the rewind happens (a rewind is not a turn); subsequent turns start cleanly ready → working → ready from the rewound point; and — depending on whether the agent rewinds in place or forks — either the SAME session_id is retained (in-place) or a linked NEW session_id appears (fork).

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode's interactive TUI ships /undo and /redo with git-snapshot semantics: /undo reverts the conversation to a previous message AND restores the working-tree files from a git snapshot, removing the subsequent messages from the SQLite session; /redo re-applies them. This is the strongest form of the canonical checkpoint primitive (conversation AND files restored), stronger than claudecode's in-place transcript rewind or pi's in-memory leaf-pointer move, and unlike codex which forks to a new thread. opencode rewinds IN PLACE on the same ses_… (the SQLite session is mutated: subsequent messages removed, so the row shrinks then grows again on the next turn). The capability exists in the agent.

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store via ComputeMetrics, keyed on the same ses_… session id. The state-correctness observables — session stays ready across the rewind, the post-rewind turn drives a clean ready → working → ready arc on the same ses_… — are exactly the read-from-store signals the basic-turn and multi-turn opencode cells already prove the daemon surfaces live. daemon_capability is therefore assessed full, but UNTESTED LIVE: the message-removal-then-regrowth (transcript shrink then grow) is a store-mutation shape no other opencode cell exercises, and whether the daemon cleanly re-reads a session whose message count went DOWN (rather than monotonically up) has not been captured. The live recording (once a driver exists) is the authority; this is the most likely place an observability_correction would land for this cell.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: slash-command)

The opencode interactive driver (.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh) drives opencode through BLOCKING headless `opencode run` subprocesses and implements ONLY three step types in its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch: `send` (launches an `opencode run` subprocess and WAITS for it to complete), `wait_turn` (a no-op — `opencode run` already blocks until the turn ends), and `sleep`. There is NO step that delivers an in-TUI slash command.

Two independent blockers, both proven this sweep:

1. **Headless `opencode run` ignores in-TUI slash commands.** A `send`-shaped recipe that sends `/undo` (or `/redo`) LINTS in-grammar but is a semantic no-op. CONFIRMED THIS SWEEP (matching the session-reset probe of 2026-05-26): headless `opencode run` does NOT process in-TUI slash commands — it stores them as literal user messages, proven with both inline `/model` and `/new`. `/undo` and `/redo` are the same class of in-TUI slash command: `opencode run "/undo"` would store the literal text `/undo` as a normal user message and run a turn, NOT rewind the session. Slash commands are a live-TUI primitive, not a headless-`run` primitive.

2. **No live-REPL session-mutation handling.** Even if a slash command could be delivered, the driver runs each turn as an independent blocking subprocess; it has no live REPL to hold open across an /undo, and no step to observe the in-place transcript shrink-then-grow on the same ses_….

### Not a false gap (and not caught by recipe-lint)

The recipe is left `applicable:false` with no `script`, so there is no step-TYPE for recipe-lint.sh to reject — the gap is the absence of a way to deliver an in-TUI slash command against a live, held-open session. /undo + /redo are fundamentally live-TUI primitives; there is no send-only equivalent, because headless `opencode run` treats `/undo` as prompt text (proven this sweep). This mirrors the opencode session-reset gap:slash-command, user-esc-interrupt gap:interrupt, and model-switch-midsession gap:model-select cells, which recipe-lint likewise could not catch.

## What would unblock it

Replace or augment drive-opencode-interactive.sh with a LIVE-REPL driver (analogous to the tmux-based claudecode/pi/aider drivers): hold the opencode TUI open, run two turns, deliver /undo to rewind to the turn-1 checkpoint, then continue with a third turn — all against the SAME held-open process so the in-place rewind (SQLite message removal then regrowth) is observable on the same ses_…. Once the driver can deliver a slash command to a live session, the cell becomes recordable as the in-place rewind shape this spec documents; the daemon's store-read behavior across the message-count decrease is the one thing to verify against the recording.

## Matrix drift

The coverage matrix should record opencode/checkpoint-rewind as agent_supports:yes (opencode ships /undo + /redo with git-snapshot semantics — stronger than claudecode's in-place rewind), driver_capability:gap:slash-command (no in-TUI slash delivery against a live session), daemon observability assessed full but untested live (the transcript shrink-then-grow store mutation is uncaptured).

## Confidence

0.7: the driver grammar (`send|wait_turn|sleep`, blocking `opencode run` per send, no slash step) is read directly from the live driver source; the headless-slash no-op is the CONFIRMED sweep finding (inline /model and /new both stored as literal user text) and /undo+/redo are the same in-TUI slash-command class; opencode's /undo+/redo git-snapshot rewind is per opencode docs. Not higher because the unblocked recording — a live /undo mutating the SQLite session and the daemon re-reading the shrunk-then-regrown session on the same ses_… — has not been captured, and the daemon's behavior across a message-count DECREASE is untested.
caveats: Not recordable today: /undo and /redo are in-TUI slash commands; headless `opencode run` stores them as literal user text (no rewind), and drive-opencode-interactive.sh has no slash-command step against a live held-open session. gap:slash-command — a developer task to add a live-REPL opencode driver.

daemon_capability is assessed full but UNTESTED LIVE: opencode rewinds in place by REMOVING subsequent messages from the SQLite session (transcript shrinks then grows again on the next turn). No other opencode cell exercises a message-count DECREASE; whether the daemon cleanly re-reads a session whose store row shrank is the most likely observability_correction for this cell once recorded.

agent_supports is yes (not partial): opencode's /undo restores BOTH conversation AND working-tree files via a git snapshot — the full canonical checkpoint primitive, stronger than claudecode's conversation-only in-place rewind or pi's leaf-pointer move.

sources.note: driver implements send (blocking `opencode run`) / wait_turn (no-op) / sleep only; no slash-command step — case "$TYPE" dispatch lines 132-159

opencode Source is ProcessOwnedStore{Reader: metricsReader{}} — daemon reads SQLite store via ComputeMetrics keyed on ses_…; an in-place rewind mutates the store row in place (subsequent messages removed)
sibling opencode gap:slash-command cell — same root cause: headless `opencode run` stores in-TUI slash commands (/new, /model) as literal user text; needs a live-REPL driver

anchor: in-place rewind stays ready, retains same session_id, post-rewind turn is a clean ready→working→ready arc — the observable shape opencode would have (opencode also rewinds in place, additionally restoring files)
sibling driver-gap rewind cell (pi gap:keys) — documented-target spec pattern for an unrecordable in-place rewind
